items.title: NBA Offseason Trade/Free Agent Rumors 2026: Mario Hezonja return rumors, plus Bradley Beal, Anthony Davis
items.summary: While the NBA world impatiently waits — and a lot of free agent action is held up — until LeBron James announces his decision on his next team, rumors are still flying around. Here is some of the latest free agency chatter around the Association.Mario Hezonja drawing interestThe last time we saw Mario Hezonja in the NBA was with Portland during the bubble, a season where he came off the bench and averaged 4.8 points per game. Since then, the former No. 5 pick has played and starred for European powerhouse Real Madrid, but now there is buzz about an NBA return at age 11. Marc Stein of The Stein Line has been in front of this story.Latest on former No. 5 overall pick Mario Hezonja per league sources: Hezonja indeed expected to rejoin the NBA for the first time since 2019-20. Multiple NBA teams have shown interest in the Real Madrid forward. List said to include teams also pursuing LeBron…

items.title: Submit your questions for The Pindown: a Detroit Pistons Podcast
items.summary: Send in your questions now for this week’s episode of The Pindown: a Detroit Pistons Podcast to discuss everything Pistons. Submit your question in the comments section here or on Twitter to @TheRealWesD3 and/or @BobbyBuckets313. If you’re on BlueSky, you can also send to @bobbybuckets313.bksy.social.Join us live on Thursday afternoon for the show where we wrap up Summer League and discuss what’s next in Detroit’s offseason. Where do Jalen Duren contract talks stand? Is Detroit showing interest in DeMar DeRozan? What does the depth chart look like right now?Plus, The Pindown has a phone line where you can leave a message and hear your voice on the show. Call (313) 355-2717 and leave us a voicemail with your question. Please try to keep the message around 45 seconds or less so we can fit everyone into the show.The podcast will be uploaded to all audio platforms…

items.title: Teen paid $3 for Wilt Chamberlain Lakers jacket at thrift store. It sold for almost $90,000 at auction
items.summary: Lakers' star Wilt Chamberlain played his final NBA season in 1972-73. The warmup jacket he was wearing in this photo is believed to be the same one that was found at a Goodwill in January and eventually auctioned for nearly $90,000. (Anonymous / Associated Press)A Portland teenager found a random old Lakers jacket in a Goodwill bin in January.He paid $3.07 for it.Seven months later, the item sold at auction for $89,600.Turns out the old jacket wasn’t so random after all. Verified by Sotheby’s auction house, it was a warmup jacket worn by NBA legend Wilt Chamberlain at least three times. That includes during the 1972 NBA Finals in which the Lakers defeated the New York Knicks in five games and Chamberlain was named the series MVP.Read more:Rare Kobe Bryant trading card 'at the top of every wish list' sells for record sum“This is Truly a once in a lifetime find,” Quinn…

items.title: Victor Wembanyama explains decision to take less money from Spurs in $252 million extension
items.summary: San Antonio Spurs superstar Victor Wembanyama doesn't want money to be the reason his team falls short of a championship. Because of that, Wembanyama took less in his new extension with the Spurs. That decision came as a bit of a surprise, as Wembanyama was set to earn a ludicrous amount of money after winning the Rookie of the Year and Defensive Player of the Year awards, and making two All-Star teams in his first three NBA seasons.For the first time since signing that deal, Wembanyama explained why he took less than the supermax, telling The Athletic it's all about keeping the team in a good spot. "The reality of today's NBA is that often, the potential of teams is not fulfilled because of money. I just want to make sure it's never our case."Though Wembanyama didn't fully maximize his future earnings with the deal, he's still making a boatload of…

items.title: Victor Wembanyama discusses contract discount, says he doesn't want money to hold Spurs back
items.summary: NBA owners loved it — this is what they and Adam Silver want to see, players willingly taking a little less money. It frustrated the NBA players' union, starting with the fact it's a decision that had to be made at all.Victor Wembanyama took a discount on his contract extension with the Spurs that could save the team a little more than $50 million over five years by agreeing to only take 25% of the salary cap — still five years, $252 million — rather than the 30% he would be eligible for if he is again Defensive Player of the Year or makes an All-NBA team (thinks that feel like a lock if he plays the required 65 games next season).For the first time, Wemby discussed his decision, speaking to Jason Jones of The Athletic about the French center being named the NBA 2K27 cover athlete (along with Caitlin Clark and Derrick Rose)."We have a good team, we had a very good playoff…

items.title: Tyler Herro’s NSFW warning to Heat after shocking Giannis Antetokounmpo trade
items.summary: New Milwaukee Bucks guard Tyler Herro was in Paris when he was traded and had a stern message for the Heat the moment he learned he was being shipped out.Herro was sent to Milwaukee, his hometown team, in a blockbuster trade that sent Giannis Antetokounmpo to the Heat, and he couldn’t believe that he was being moved.“Back to the motherf–king crib, dog,” Herro said in a video posted on his YouTube channel. “Y’all did lost your motherf–king mind.”Bucks guard Tyler Herro reacts to his trade to Milwaukee on his YouTube channel. Tyler Herro/YouTubeHe appeared to be all smiles, singing verses of “The Boys Are Back in Town” by Thin Lizzy multiple times after learning of his new team.A passenger in the car with him then began singing “I’m Coming Home” by Skylar Grey in anticipation for his return to Wisconsin.“I’m ready to come home and not prove everyone wrong,” he told ESPN, “but just be…

items.title: PHOTO: Indiana Fever's Caitlin Clark featured on NBA 2K27 cover
items.summary: Caitlin Clark is the first WNBA player to be featured on a global NBA 2K cover.NBA 2K27 announced today that Clark, Victor Wembanyama abd Derrick Rose will be featured on covers of the editions, which will be will released Sept. 4. Clark will be on the deluxe cover.She’s obsessed with the work Caitlin Clark is your NBA 2K27 Deluxe Edition Cover AthletePre-order NBA 2K27 now! ➡️ https://t.co/J6pi6mirRHpic.twitter.com/Fk1UG0aLcl— NBA 2K (@NBA2K) July 22, 2026"Being on the cover of NBA 2K27 is special because this game reaches fans all over the world,” Clark said. “To be the first WNBA player on her own global cover means women's basketball gets to show up on that stage too, and I am proud to be part of that."Clark joins a handful of WNBA legends who have made NBA 2K cover appearances, including Candance Parker (2022), Diana Taurasi (2023), Sue Bird (2023), Sabrina Ionescu…

items.title: Caitlin Clark scores historic NBA 2K27 Deluxe Edition cover honor
items.summary: Caitlin Clark is officially an NBA 2K27 cover athlete.On Wednesday, NBA 2K announced the specially selected cover athletes for NBA 2K27. NBA star Victor Wembanyama will be on the cover of the Standard Edition, and Chicago Bulls legend Derrick Rose will be on the limited-time Ultra Edition. Clark, who is set to grace the cover of the Deluxe Edition, is the first WNBA player featured on her own global NBA 2K premium cover."Being on the cover of NBA 2K27 is special because this game reaches fans all over the world,” said Clark via release. “To be the first WNBA player on her own global cover means women's basketball gets to show up on that stage too, and I am proud to be part of that."She’s obsessed with the work 💭🏀Caitlin Clark is your NBA 2K27 Deluxe Edition Cover AthletePre-order NBA 2K27 now! ➡️ https://t.co/3HbX2GhFQ5pic.twitter.com/JTeXXVpEkw— WNBA (@WNBA) July 22,…
